title: New Deadline Looms for U.S. and Iran as Truce Wavers
summary: Fractures were already emerging in the limited cease-fire. Vice President JD Vance will lead a U.S. delegation in talks this weekend.

title: After Cease-Fire, Iranians Are Left to Pick Up the Pieces
summary: Hours after a tenuous pause to a nearly six-week war, Iranians expressed relief and trepidation about the future. Some fear the government will crack down on its domestic critics.

title: Trump Finds Himself With Fragile Iran Cease-Fire After the Scramble to Make a Deal
summary: After careening from one diplomatic extreme to another, President Trump finds himself with a fragile deal that is already showing signs of fraying.

title: Gilgo Beach Killer’s ‘Unusual’ Deal Could Help Solve Other Murders
summary: Rex Heuermann agreed to talk to behavioral analysts at the F.B.I. when he pleaded guilty. The unit has interviewed Ted Bundy, John Wayne Gacy and Richard Speck.

title: In 20 Minutes, Gilgo Beach Killer Admits to Murdering 8 Women
summary: As he pleaded guilty on Wednesday, Rex Heuermann gave the same one-word answer each time he was asked how he killed one of his victims.

title: White House Secures Foreign Steel for Trump’s Ballroom Project
summary: ArcelorMittal, a European steel maker, is donating tens of millions of dollars of foreign steel for President Trump’s new ballroom.

title: Bondi Won’t Appear on Capitol Hill for Scheduled Epstein Deposition
summary: Pam Bondi had already been working to avoid testifying before she was fired as attorney general. The House Oversight Committee said she would not honor her subpoena because she was no longer in the post.

title: Federal Court Denies Anthropic’s Motion to Lift ‘Supply Chain Risk’ Label
summary: The ruling was a setback for the artificial intelligence start-up in its battle with the Defense Department over the use of A.I. in warfare.

title: Trump Administration Investigating L.A. Schools’ Gender Disclosure Policies
summary: The investigation into the nation’s second-largest school district was prompted by a lawsuit from parents who say the policies contributed to their child’s death.

title: U.S. Fertility Rates Drop to Another Record Low
summary: The fertility rate has been falling since 2007, in large part because of a plunge among teenagers.

title: An Audacious $724 Million Building Reinvents LACMA
summary: Two decades in the making, the David Geffen Galleries will offer an unconventional approach to art history and cement the director Michael Govan’s legacy.

title: No, Britain Is Not Having a Christian Revival
summary: A study said church attendance had soared among British young people, a trend reversal that excited religious conservatives around the world. Turns out it wasn’t true.

title: On Truth Social, Trump Supporters Fume About Iran War
summary: A growing chorus of disaffected Trump supporters is sounding off in the replies to his posts on the social media platform he founded.

title: Blue Collar Work Has Plateaued, Narrowing Options for Young Workers
summary: Skilled electricians, plumbers and factory workers are in demand, but job openings have dropped.

title: ‘Ketamine Queen’ Sentenced to 15 Years in Matthew Perry’s Overdose Death
summary: The sentence for Jasveen Sangha, who prosecutors say was known to customers as the Ketamine Queen, is the stiffest yet for those charged in the “Friends” star’s death.

title: Hasan Piker Can Stream, but Can He Stump?
summary: The progressive and popular Twitch streamer, who is controversial with some Democrats, tests his political influence on the 2026 campaign trail.

title: Clean Energy Team Wins Salt River Project Election in Arizona
summary: Proponents of renewable power will control the Phoenix area utility’s policymaking for the first time after they won an unusually contentious race that drew attention from national groups.

title: No Charges for Wisconsin Mayor Who Removed Ballot Drop Box in 2024
summary: A special prosecutor said a review of the incident found there was not sufficient evidence to charge Doug Diny, the mayor of Wausau.

title: Who Is Funding the 2026 Midterm Elections? A Lot of the Money Is Untraceable.
summary: A lot of the money flowing into the political system is ultimately untraceable.

title: Mamdani Acknowledges ‘Troubling’ Job Losses in 100 Days’ Interview
summary: Mayor Zohran Mamdani is focused on economic justice. Some budget watchdogs and business leaders want him to concentrate on economic development.

title: Jim Whittaker, First American to Reach Everest’s Summit, Dies at 97
summary: As an executive with the outdoor-supply retailer REI and an experienced climber, he conquered Mount Everest in 1963, when fewer than 10 people were known to have done so.

title: Trump Wants Orban to Win. That’s Why He Might Not.
summary: Viktor Orban’s 16-year rule could be coming to a close.

title: NASA Flew by the Moon, but Behind the Scenes, Its Science Is a Chaotic Mess
summary: Without science, the stunning images of Earth from space are only pretty pictures.

title: Stephen Colbert Skewers Trump’s ‘Double-Sided’ Cease-Fire
summary: “The Late Show” host asked if being double-sided wasn’t a prerequisite: “I believe there’s a word for a single-sided cease-fire and it’s ‘murder.’”

title: Hawaii Doctor Convicted of Attempted Manslaughter in Attack on Wife
summary: Gerhardt Konig, 47, an anesthesiologist from Maui, attacked his wife, Arielle Konig, on a hiking trail near Honolulu, prosecutors said. He claimed it was self-defense.

title: Parts of Hawaii Face a Foot or More of Rain From Yet Another Storm
summary: Last month was one of the wettest Marches on record for the state, leading to widespread flooding and evacuations. More rain is hitting this week.

title: Martin Gugino, Activist Shoved by Buffalo Police at 2020 Protest, Dies
summary: Mr. Gugino, 81, had filed a lawsuit in 2021 against the city of Buffalo and members of its police force after officers fractured his skull at a Black Lives Matter protest.

title: Half of Gen Z Uses AI, but Their Feelings Are Souring, Study Shows
summary: A new study from Gallup found that young adults have grown less hopeful and more angry about artificial intelligence.
